description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has been asked to provide the
    exact total amount of cloud expenditure for the month “2023-07”.
    A SQLite database has already been exported from the company’s
    billing pipeline and stored on the workstation.

    1.  Database location  
        • File path: /home/user/finops/cloud_spend.db

    2.  Relevant schema (already present)  
        • Table name: usage  
        • Columns & types  
          – id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
          – service     TEXT  
          – month       TEXT  (values such as “2023-07”, “2023-06”, …)  
          – cost        REAL  (USD cost for that row)

    3.  Your task  
        Run ONE (1) SQLite-CLI command (plus ordinary shell
        redirection, if you wish) that:  
        a. Calculates the total of the “cost” column for rows where
           month = "2023-07".  
        b. Prints that single figure in the following exact format
           (no extra spaces or thousands separators):  

               Total cost for 2023-07: $<value_with_two_decimal_places>

           – The string must start with “Total cost for 2023-07: $”.  
           – The numeric value must always show exactly two digits after
             the decimal point (e.g. 1234.50, 1024.57, 0.00).  
        c. Redirects (or otherwise writes) the output into the log file
           /home/user/finops/july_2023_total_cost.log  
           so that the file contains exactly one line, followed by a
           single trailing newline character.

    4.  Verification rules used by the automated tester  
        • /home/user/finops/july_2023_total_cost.log must exist.  
        • The file must be world-readable (0644 or less restrictive).  
        • It must contain exactly one line with the precise format
          described above, including the colon, dollar sign and two
          decimal places, and nothing else.  
        • The numeric value must match the SUM(cost) for month
          “2023-07” recorded in /home/user/finops/cloud_spend.db.

    5.  Restrictions  
        • Your solution must be produced via a single direct invocation
          of the sqlite3 CLI (any command-line options you like) plus
          ordinary redirection if needed.  
        • Do not edit, move, or rename the database file.  
        • Do not create any additional helper scripts—just use the
          terminal.

    Reminder: only one concise command is expected; after running it,
    confirm that /home/user/finops/july_2023_total_cost.log looks
    exactly as specified.
truth:
Initial filesystem state
    ------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/finops           (mode 0755)
      • /home/user/finops/cloud_spend.db   (mode 0644)

    SQLite details
    --------------
    Schema:
        CREATE TABLE usage(
            id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            service TEXT,
            month   TEXT,
            cost    REAL
        );

    Seed data already present in /home/user/finops/cloud_spend.db:
        INSERT INTO usage (id, service, month, cost) VALUES
            (1, 'Compute Engine', '2023-07', 523.23),
            (2, 'Cloud Storage',  '2023-07', 123.45),
            (3, 'BigQuery',       '2023-07', 300.00),
            (4, 'Compute Engine', '2023-06', 200.00),
            (5, 'BigQuery',       '2023-07',  77.89);

    Expected calculation
    --------------------
        SELECT SUM(cost) FROM usage WHERE month='2023-07';
        --> 1024.57

    Expected final artefact
    -----------------------
    File: /home/user/finops/july_2023_total_cost.log (mode 0644)
    Exact content (including trailing newline):
        Total cost for 2023-07: $1024.57\n
